title: optimal code ko ache se explain karna pleae bataooo mujhe

transcript.text: optimal code ko ache se explain karna pleae bataooo mujhe

blocks.md:
Bilkul! Chalo `Solution` class ko line-by-line, ekdum aaram se samajhte hain — Hinglish mein, taaki narrate bhi kar sako interview mein. 🙂

Main code ko **6 hisso** mein toड़ता hoon. Har hisse ka *kya kar raha hai* aur *kyun* dono.

---

## Pura code ek nazar mein

```python
class Solution:
    def optimalBST(self, words, frequency):
        n = len(words)
        if n == 0:
            return None

        # PART 1: prefix sums
        prefix = [0] * (n + 1)
        for idx in range(n):
            prefix[idx + 1] = prefix[idx] + frequency[idx]
        def slice_sum(i, j):
            return prefix[j + 1] - prefix[i]

        # PART 2: tables
        INF = float("inf")
        dp = [[0] * n for _ in range(n)]
        root = [[0] * n for _ in range(n)]

        # PART 3: base case
        for i in range(n):
            dp[i][i] = frequency[i]
            root[i][i] = i

        # PART 4: main DP
        for length in range(2, n + 1):
            for i in range(0, n - length + 1):
                j = i + length - 1
                best = INF
                for r in range(i, j + 1):
                    left = dp[i][r - 1] if r > i else 0
                    right = dp[r + 1][j] if r < j else 0
                    cost = left + right
                    if cost < best:
                        best = cost
                        root[i][j] = r
                dp[i][j] = best + slice_sum(i, j)

        # PART 5: rebuild tree
        def build(i, j):
            if i > j:
                return None
            r = root[i][j]
            node = TreeNode(words[r])
            node.left = build(i, r - 1)
            node.right = build(r + 1, j)
            return node

        self.min_cost = dp[0][n - 1]
        return build(0, n - 1)
```

---

## Sabse pehle — 2 cheezein dimaag mein fix kar lo

Ye do tables hain, **yahi pura solution hai**:

```
dp[i][j]   = words[i..j] slice ka MINIMUM total search cost
root[i][j] = us slice ke liye konsa word ROOT banaya (taaki tree wapas bana sakein)
```

> 💬 "Main do tables rakhta hoon — `dp` mein har slice ka best cost, aur `root` mein har slice ka chosen root. `dp` se answer milta hai, `root` se actual tree."

**Slice matlab kya?** Kyunki words sorted hain, BST ka koi bhi subtree hamesha ek **continuous tukda** hoga — jaise `words[2..5]`. Isiliye sirf `i` aur `j` (start aur end) se har sub-problem describe ho jaata hai. Bas yahi interval DP ki jaan hai.

---

## PART 1 — Prefix sum (slice ka frequency total O(1) mein)

```python
prefix = [0] * (n + 1)
for idx in range(n):
    prefix[idx + 1] = prefix[idx] + frequency[idx]
def slice_sum(i, j):
    return prefix[j + 1] - prefix[i]
```

Yaad hai recurrence mein `+ sum(freq[i..j])` aata tha? Wo har slice ke liye baar-baar chahiye. Agar har baar loop laga ke sum karein toh extra time lagega. Isliye **ek baar prefix sum bana lo**, phir koi bhi slice ka sum subtraction se aa jaata hai.

Example `freq = [30, 10, 40]`:
```
prefix = [0, 30, 40, 80]
          ↑   ↑   ↑   ↑
        idx0  a  a+b a+b+c

slice_sum(0, 2) = prefix[3] - prefix[0] = 80 - 0 = 80   ✓ (apple+banana+cherry)
slice_sum(1, 2) = prefix[3] - prefix[1] = 80 - 30 = 50  ✓ (banana+cherry)
```

> 💬 "Prefix sum isliye taaki kisi bhi slice ka frequency total O(1) mein mile — har baar dobara add na karna pade."

---

## PART 2 + PART 3 — Tables banao, base case bharo

```python
dp = [[0] * n for _ in range(n)]
root = [[0] * n for _ in range(n)]

for i in range(n):
    dp[i][i] = frequency[i]   # akela word
    root[i][i] = i
```

**Base case = sabse chhoti slice = ek akela word.** Ek akela word hamesha root par baithta hai → depth 1 → uska cost = uski apni frequency. Aur uska root khud hi hai.

```
dp[apple][apple]   = 30
dp[banana][banana] = 10
dp[cherry][cherry] = 40
```

> 💬 "Sabse pehle single-word slices — akela word matlab depth 1, toh cost bas uski frequency."

---

## PART 4 — Asli DP (yahi dil hai ❤️)

```python
for length in range(2, n + 1):          # slice ki lambai: 2, 3, 4 ... n
    for i in range(0, n - length + 1):  # slice ka start
        j = i + length - 1              # slice ka end
        best = INF
        for r in range(i, j + 1):       # har word ko root try karo
            left  = dp[i][r - 1] if r > i else 0
            right = dp[r + 1][j] if r < j else 0
            cost = left + right
            if cost < best:
                best = cost
                root[i][j] = r
        dp[i][j] = best + slice_sum(i, j)
```

Ye 3 nested loops hain — ek-ek karke samjho:

**Loop 1 — `length`:** Hum slices ko **chhoti se badi** order mein bharte hain. Pehle saari length-2 slices, phir length-3, ... Kyun? Kyunki length-3 ka answer length-2 aur length-1 ke answers par depend karta hai — wo **pehle se ready hone chahiye**. Isiliye chhoti pehle.

**Loop 2 — `i`:** slice kaha se start hoti hai. `j = i + length - 1` se end nikal aata hai.

**Loop 3 — `r` (sabse important):** is slice ke har word ko baari-baari **root** banao.
- Agar `r` root hai → uske left mein `[i..r-1]` ka best subtree, right mein `[r+1..j]` ka best subtree.
- Ye dono answer `dp` mein **pehle se** bhare pade hain (chhoti slices), toh seedha utha lo.

```python
left  = dp[i][r - 1] if r > i else 0   # ⚠️ agar r == i, left khaali hai → 0
right = dp[r + 1][j] if r < j else 0   # ⚠️ agar r == j, right khaali hai → 0
```
Ye `if...else 0` wala guard zaroori hai — agar root slice ke kinare par hai toh ek side khaali hoti hai, uska cost 0.

**`cost = left + right`** — sirf dono subtrees ka cost compare karo, jo root sabse kam de wo jeet gaya, uska index `root[i][j]` mein save kar do.

**Sabse subtle line:**
```python
dp[i][j] = best + slice_sum(i, j)
```
Loop ke andar humne sirf `left + right` compare kiya. **`slice_sum` ek hi baar, ant mein, add karte hain.** Kyun? Kyunki root choose karte hi pure slice ke saare nodes ek level neeche chale jaate hain → har node ka +1 comparison → total extra = us slice ke saari frequencies ka sum. Aur ye penalty har root ke liye **same** hai, isiliye comparison ko affect nahi karti — bas ant mein chipka do.

> 💬 "Andar wale loop mein main sirf left+right minimize karta hoon. Slice ka frequency sum sirf ek baar bahar add karta hoon — kyunki root banane se pura slice ek level neeche girta hai, aur wo penalty har root ke liye barabar hai."

**Mini-trace, slice `[apple, banana]`, slice_sum = 40:**
```
r = apple  → left 0 + right dp[banana]=10 = 10   ✓ jeet
r = banana → left dp[apple]=30 + right 0  = 30
best = 10, root = apple
dp[apple,banana] = 10 + 40 = 50
```

---

## PART 5 — Tree wapas banao (`root` table se)

```python
def build(i, j):
    if i > j:                      # khaali slice → koi node nahi
        return None
    r = root[i][j]                 # is slice ka chosen root
    node = TreeNode(words[r])
    node.left  = build(i, r - 1)   # left mein left-slice ka tree
    node.right = build(r + 1, j)   # right mein right-slice ka tree
    return node
```

DP ne sirf cost nikala, lekin question **tree** maangta hai. Isiliye humne `root[i][j]` save kiya tha. Ab uska use karke recursively tree bana lete hain:
- Pure slice `[0..n-1]` ka root nikalo → wahi tree ka root.
- Uske left mein `[i..r-1]` ka root recurse karke lagao, right mein `[r+1..j]` ka.
- `i > j` ho jaaye toh khaali → `None`.

```
root[0..2] = cherry → cherry top par
  build(left  [apple,banana]) → root apple → uska right banana
  build(right [empty])        → None

        cherry
        /
     apple
         \
       banana       ✓ cost 130
```

> 💬 "DP ne cost diya, par mujhe tree chahiye. Isiliye maine har slice ka chosen root store kiya tha — ab usse recursively poora tree bana leta hoon."

---

## Ek line mein puri kahani

> 💬 "Har slice ke liye har root try karo, cost = left ka best + right ka best + slice ka frequency-sum. Chhoti slices pehle bharo, phir badi. `root` table se asli tree wapas bana lo. Time O(n³), space O(n²)."

Kahin bhi atka ho — kisi ek loop ya line par — bolo, main usi par aur deep chala jaata hoon. 👍
